user_query:
Please read input/policy.yaml and input/conversation.jsonl, then simulate a three-stage contextual recall orchestrator across the full conversation and produce two artifacts:

1) Write output/recall_trace.jsonl where each line is a JSON object for the corresponding input turn with the following shape:
- input: { text, intent (if provided), entities (from the turn), pre_execution (boolean) }
- recallDecision: { needed (boolean), mode (one of: preflight_query | continuation_query | entity_query | constraint_query), reason (short string) }
- recallResult: { status (one of: not_needed | queried_no_hits | queried_low_confidence | queried_success | query_failed), mode, items (array) }
- compressed: { totalCount, compressedCount, items (length must be <= 5) }
- log: a single structured log line string capturing at least: intent, recall_mode, recall_status, injected_item_count, pre_execution_gate (boolean)

Use these rules from the policy:
- Stage 1 (Session preflight): On the first turn marked new_session: true, set recallDecision.needed = true and mode = preflight_query even if the text is casual; generate a compact session digest (your recallResult items) and then compress to <= 5 items.
- Stage 2 (Triggered recall):
  - If the text indicates a continuation (contains words like "resume", "previous", "last time"), choose mode = continuation_query.
  - If entities[] is non-empty and there is no continuation or execution/design trigger, choose mode = entity_query.
  - If the intent indicates design/change/implementation or the text contains execution-like verbs (edit, modify, implement, design, change), choose mode = constraint_query.
  - Skip recall (needed = false) for generic Q&A or casual chat when self-contained and not dependent on prior context.
  - When multiple triggers apply, use this precedence: continuation_query > entity_query > constraint_query.
- Stage 3 (Pre-execution gate): For any turn with pre_execution: true in the input, ensure the structured log reflects pre_execution_gate: true.
- Status tracking: If recallDecision.needed is false, set recallResult.status = not_needed and items can be empty; otherwise use an appropriate queried_* status.
- Compression and limits: Compressed.items must be <= 5 across all turns.

2) Write output/review.md containing a brief analysis (150–300 words) of when recall was invoked vs. skipped for this conversation, how compression/deduplication kept context bounded, how continuation/entity/execution triggers were applied, and at least 3 concrete improvement suggestions for the recall policy. Make the review actionable and clear.

Important:
- Use only English in outputs.
- Do not invent external files—use only the two inputs provided.
- Ensure output/recall_trace.jsonl has exactly the same number of lines as input/conversation.jsonl, preserving turn order.
- When a turn’s pre_execution is true, reflect that in the log and prefer constraint_query for design/execution-like requests.
- When a turn is a self-contained generic question, no recall should be triggered.
